##NAMES=Magnesiochlorophoenicite
##RRUFFID=R140719
##IDEAL CHEMISTRY=Mg_3_Zn_2_(AsO_4_)(OH,O)_6_
##LOCALITY=Sterling mine, Sterling Hill, Ogdensburg, Franklin Mining District, Sussex County, New Jersey, USA
##OWNER=RRUFF
##SOURCE=Rock Currier
##DESCRIPTION=Radial tufts of vitreous white acicular crystals on typical Franklin Hill zinc ore. The crystals are twinned by a 2-fold axis parallel to a*.
##STATUS=The identification of this mineral has been confirmed only by single crystal X-ray diffraction
##URL=https://www.rruff.net/odr/rruff_sample/R140719
##DIFFRACTION SAMPLE DESCRIPTION=Single crystal, powder profile is calculated
##CELL PARAMETERS=a: 23.149 b: 3.2908 c: 7.4029 alpha: 90 beta: 106.371 gamma: 90 volume: 541.08 crystal system: monoclinic
##FILETYPE=Raman Processed
##RAMAN WAVELENGTH=780
